title: Objection: 'Just send me an email'
description: The most common cold-call brush-off. Handle it without sounding pushy or giving up.
buyer_context: You're 45 seconds into a cold call. The buyer is a Director of Sales at a mid-market company. He's been cordial but wants to end the call. He says: 'You know what, just send me an email and I'll take a look when I have time.'
user_goal: Either (a) earn a proper 10-minute discovery conversation in the next week, OR (b) get a concrete reason why now isn't the right time. Don't just agree to send an email — that almost always goes nowhere.
challenge_prompt: Buyer: 'You know what, just send me an email and I'll take a look when I have time.' How do you respond?

ideal_behaviors: Acknowledge the request warmly without immediately caving
Reframe: note that emails get buried, propose a specific short call instead
Offer a concrete time window ('15 minutes Thursday at 2 or Friday at 10?')
Tie the ask back to a specific value or insight — why it's worth 15 min
If they still push back, ask one revealing question instead of pitching harder
common_mistakes: Agreeing immediately: 'Sure, I'll send it over!' (call is dead)
Vague next step: 'Let me follow up next week' (no specifics, no commitment)
Launching into pitch to 'change their mind'
Not asking anything that reveals priority or timing
